title: SOFI Stock Slides As Long-Term Growth Story Stays Intact
text: SoFi Technologies stock slid despite strong earnings results as analyst attention shifted to execution risks and valuation concerns. The company reported Q1 2026 results showing revenue growth of 41% to a record $1.1 billion and net income more than doubling to $166.7 million. CEO Anthony Noto noted the company achieved its 18th consecutive quarter of the Rule of 40 with a score of 72, reflecting 41% revenue growth and 31% EBITDA margins. Despite these strong metrics, the stock declined approximately 13% post-earnings as investors digested concerns about declining technology platform revenue (down 27% YoY due to a customer departure) and the company's conservative guidance despite beating expectations. Analyst sentiment turned negative following Q1 with multiple price target reductions: Goldman Sachs cut from $20 to $17 with Neutral, Morgan Stanley slashed to $16 with Underweight, and Truist cut to $17. However, William Blair maintained an Outperform rating, noting SoFi is steadily taking market share from traditional banks. The market's focus shifted from growth headlines to execution risks in lending and technology segments.
